mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in housing units sold signals a strengthening demand for housing, which is positively correlated with S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising housing units sold are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month decline in industrial production signals a weakening supply chain, which is negatively correlated with S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with declining industrial production are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month increase in the federal funds rate signals a tightening monetary cycle that compresses equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with rising federal funds rate are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in initial unemployment claims signals a weakening labor market, which is negatively correlated with S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising initial unemployment claims are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real GDP signals a strengthening demand for goods and services, which is positively correlated with S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with rising real GDP are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month decline in industrial production and a 1-month increase in housing units sold signals a weakening supply chain and strengthening demand for housing, which is negatively correlated with S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with declining industrial production and rising housing units sold are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month increase in the federal funds rate and a 2-month decline in real GDP signals a tightening monetary cycle and weakening demand for goods and services, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with rising federal funds rate and declining real GDP are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in initial unemployment claims and a 1-month decline in payroll employment signals a weakening labor market and tightening labor supply, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising initial unemployment claims and declining payroll employment are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
